tree.text: Middle School Daily Life in China (Advanced)

children.text: Dear Linda, I'm glad to write this email to introduce the middle school students' daily life in China to you.
Let me first introduce myself. My name is Liu Bo, and I am a 13-year-old boy currently studying in Grade 7.
As for my appearance, I am quite tall for my age, with short black hair and a pair of bright, big eyes.
When I'm not busy with my studies, I enjoy various hobbies.
children.highlight: When I'm not busy with my studies,

children.text: For example, reading is one of my favorite pastimes, as it allows me to explore different worlds.
children.highlight: For example, as it allows me to explore different worlds.

children.text: In addition, I am also keen on playing basketball with my friends, which helps me stay active and healthy.
children.highlight: In addition, which helps me stay active and healthy.

children.text: A typical school day for me begins quite early.
children.highlight: A typical school day for me begins quite early.

children.text: I usually wake up at 6:30 AM, and after a quick breakfast, I head to school by 7:15 AM.
children.highlight: I usually wake up at 6:30 AM, and after a quick breakfast, I head to school by 7:15 AM.

children.text: We have classes until noon, and then we have lunch at school, which is very convenient.
children.highlight: and then, which is very convenient.

children.text: After school finishes at 5:00 PM, I usually go home to do my homework. Although the homework can sometimes be challenging, I always try my best.
children.highlight: After school finishes at 5:00 PM, Although the homework can sometimes be challenging, I always try my best.

children.text: Finally, after dinner, I relax for a bit before going to bed, preparing for another busy day.
children.highlight: Finally, preparing for another busy day.
